description: Figure 16 presents a response diagram evaluating the prediction accuracy of the established GA-Extra Trees yield strength (YS) model for medium-manganese low-density steels. The scatter plot compares predicted YS values (y-axis, Predicted Values/MPa) against experimentally measured YS values (x-axis, Actual Values/MPa) for six independent validation data points collected from literature studies. The six orange data points span an actual yield strength range from approximately 600 MPa to 1650 MPa, covering two different alloy compositions and various processing conditions. The red dashed diagonal line represents the ideal 1:1 prediction reference. The data points cluster tightly around this line, indicating excellent agreement between predicted and measured values. According to the accompanying text, the average prediction error for these six literature datasets is 1.84%, which is close to the 1.56% error obtained from direct experimental verification on Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel. This figure thereby demonstrates the strong generalization capability and robustness of the multi-mechanism strengthening-based machine learning model across different medium-manganese low-density steel compositions and processing histories.
